calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Arthur Liu for Petitioner.

The Court raised the issue of Mr. Fong making distribution that was not authorized and questions why the Court should ratify it. Mr. Liu stated that this is not contested, that there is only one Heir and that he does not know how to unwrap what has happened. The Court raised the issue of the Representative taking the distributions back and stated that the Representative has to be accountable to the Court. The Court also expressed confusion with language that indicates that the Representative is retaining sufficient funds to close the estate, but the order containing language indicating that the balance of the estate is zero. Mr. Liu stated that he can amend the petition to make sure it complies with the Examiner's notes and get it cleaned up, that he can get it filed in within a week, that the house was sold and the Client received the proceeds, and that he believes Mr. Ong executed an Executor's deed to his Client. Mr. Liu also stated that the 16th Street property has been sold by Mr. Leung, that $20,000.00 has been paid in Attorney's fees, that about the same amount was paid in Administrator's fees and that he is aware that the Probate Code prohibits agreements for different amounts in fees that are more than what the code provides. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-22-2020 Continued To Mar-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-08-2020 Continued To Jan-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-27-2019 Continued To Jan-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Arthur Liu for Petitioner.

Mr. Liu requested a continuance to the end of the year stating that he is waiting for the inventory and appraisal, that it is done and that it is a matter of the Probate Referee getting the paper work back to him. The Amended Accounting Petition Re: Final Distribution is continued to January 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Continued To Nov-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Arthur J. Liu for Leung Sik Kuen (Petitioner/Beneficiary).

Mr. Liu stated that his client is the petitioner. The Court stated that it was Mr. Leung's petition, that the verification was by Mr. Ong, and that Mr. Ong is appointed as administrator. Mr. Liu stated that Mr. Ong is a long time friend of his client. Mr. Liu then confirmed for the Court that he had received the probate examiner's notes. The Court stated that an Inventory & Appraisal was not on file, that it wants an Inventory & Appraisal filed, and that without it there is no way to set the statutory fees. Mr. Liu stated that the fees were taken care of already. The Court stated the it would still need a proper petition before the Court. The Court addressed the request to waive the Inventory & Appraisal stating that it is not the proper procedure under the Probate Code. The Court also stated stated that the distribution was done without a Court order, that there are basic elements of Probate, that without the necessary information that we can't do our jobs, that it needs an Inventory & Appraisal or petition before the court, and that distributions need prior authorization. Mr Liu stated that the matter is not contested, that his client is the sole beneficiary, that his client has already sold the house, and that he wasn't sure how they would do and Inventory & Appraisal at this point. The Court inquired how it was held. Mr. Liu stated that there was an executor's deed to his client. The Court stated that this should not have happened and that there are concerns about unauthorized distributions. Ms. Hilton stated that the probate referee would need to do a date of death appraisal and referred Mr. Liu to the SF Superior Court Local Rules. Mr. Liu argued that an Inventory & Appraisal was not needed, that there were two accounts, and that there were cash assets of $23,000.00 & $100,000.00. The Court stated that an Inventory & Appraisal as of the date of death was to be done so that the Court can match it with the final distribution. The Court addressed Mr. Liu stating that going forward he is to follow the rules. Mr. Liu stated that the probate referee is Warren Wong. The Court stated that Mr. Wong had retired and appointed Lawrence Lee as the probate referee in this matter. The Court then continued the Amended Petition for Waiver of Accounting, Allowance of Fees and Final Distribution to November 27, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court ordered Mr. Liu to submit a proposed order appointing Lawrence Lee as the new probate referee.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-26-2019 Continued To Aug-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gerard Lam for Petitioner.

Mr. Lam stated that he is working with Attorney Arthur Liu, that he is working on the accounting and the amended petition, and that he needs more time. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to August 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-20-2019 Continued To Jun-26-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: George Ong specially appearing for Attorney Arthur Liu.

Mr. Ong requested a 30-day continuance stating that Mr. Liu is on tour of the Country with his Daughter who won the world figure skating championship. The Court noted that at the last hearing Mr. Liu indicated that he was going to have another Attorney help him with wrapping things up, and that stated that there is a lot of work that needs to be done. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to June 26, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Continued To May-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Arthur Liu for Leung Kuen (Petitioner).

Mr. Liu stated that the assets have already been distributed to his Client, that he is wrapping up the case and that he needs a little more time to do some accounting. Mr. Liu also stated that he will be out of the Country next month and will not be returning until May 13th, that they have already retained another Attorney to assist in wrapping this up and that they will have everything filed by May 20th. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to May 20, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-18-2019 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
